headline: School politics class: Kemi Badenoch announces plan to leave ECHR
body_text:
This article picked by a teacher with suggested questions is part of the Financial Times free schools access programme. Details/registration here.

Read our full range of politics picks here.

As was widely predicted, the Conservative opposition has followed Reform UK in calling for the UK to withdraw from the European Convention on Human Rights. This would give the UK more power, they argue, to protect our borders and deport illegal migrants. By contrast, the Labour government has spoken of renegotiating the convention, but would not advocate leaving it.

Click the link below to read the article and then answer the questions:

Kemi Badenoch announces plan to leave ECHR

For the Labour position see: UK’s top lawyer attacks Badenoch over call to leave European human rights treaty

Question in the style of Edexcel Politics Paper 2

TIP: The article mentions the protection of UK borders from illegal migration and the prosecution of army veterans for actions during the Northern Ireland ‘Tr
